---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: ba9347d6ff69b3a9654008e66e389dfa515c7ffc
findings:
  - { id: judged-dcj-render-split-vs-one-code-path, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-2 commits the preflight to rendering richer detail (per-AC attestation paths, derived-tree roots, excluded-commit names) directly from evidence.StoryResult/FeatureResult — detail that a real close's refusal path never prints, since close surfaces only the gate functions' coarse Reason strings. Parent dc-5 states \"the preflight report and close's refusal are one code path and their required agreement (co-3) is structural.\" Under dc-2's design the shared code path covers verdict computation only; the report and the refusal become two rendering paths whose textual content is guaranteed to differ, so co-3's agreement is structural at the verdict layer but not at the report layer, and preflight-only detail can drift from what a refusal would actually say. dc-2 discloses this as \"a rendering difference, not a second verdict-computing implementation\" but — unlike the dc-3 widening and the dc-2 ritual-step narrowing, which are explicitly flagged as tensions for seam adjudication — never names it as a deviation from dc-5's one-code-path wording, and no supersedes/exempts edge exists against closure-ergonomics#dc-5. (confidence 0.30)", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }
  - { id: judged-dcj-excluded-record-disclosure-vs-fold-authority, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-4 has the preflight explicitly name found-but-excluded (non-ancestor) commit directories, while the fold itself (records.go:90-96) silently drops them — parent dc-3 establishes that where two surfaces would disagree about record acceptance, \"the fold's rule is authoritative\" and other surfaces conform to it verbatim. A preflight that surfaces records the authoritative fold treats as nonexistent creates a second, louder account of the same resolution rule with no declared edge; dc-4 justifies it as free disclosure that changes no verdict, but does not flag the tension with dc-3's single-authority framing (dc-3 is worded around sync, so the preflight's applicability is a judgment call nobody declared). (confidence 0.15)", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/close-preflight#dc-1, spec/close-preflight#dc-2, spec/close-preflight#dc-3, spec/close-preflight#dc-4, spec/close-preflight#dc-5, spec/close-preflight#dc-6, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-1, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-2, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-3, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-4, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:0e3a2a3f2a394339efd225bcba66b81f392e4dd9f622ef7b731933c52fdb8e7b
integrity: sha256:9ab49410ccb3e27b6abe63f32730bf6e64f69a91d872412f48df0c9bbe684563
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"dcj-render-split-vs-one-code-path\",\"text\":\"Story dc-2 commits the preflight to rendering richer detail (per-AC attestation paths, derived-tree roots, excluded-commit names) directly from evidence.StoryResult/FeatureResult — detail that a real close's refusal path never prints, since close surfaces only the gate functions' coarse Reason strings. Parent dc-5 states \\\"the preflight report and close's refusal are one code path and their required agreement (co-3) is structural.\\\" Under dc-2's design the shared code path covers verdict computation only; the report and the refusal become two rendering paths whose textual content is guaranteed to differ, so co-3's agreement is structural at the verdict layer but not at the report layer, and preflight-only detail can drift from what a refusal would actually say. dc-2 discloses this as \\\"a rendering difference, not a second verdict-computing implementation\\\" but — unlike the dc-3 widening and the dc-2 ritual-step narrowing, which are explicitly flagged as tensions for seam adjudication — never names it as a deviation from dc-5's one-code-path wording, and no supersedes/exempts edge exists against closure-ergonomics#dc-5.\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/closure-ergonomics\"},{\"id\":\"dcj-excluded-record-disclosure-vs-fold-authority\",\"text\":\"Story dc-4 has the preflight explicitly name found-but-excluded (non-ancestor) commit directories, while the fold itself (records.go:90-96) silently drops them — parent dc-3 establishes that where two surfaces would disagree about record acceptance, \\\"the fold's rule is authoritative\\\" and other surfaces conform to it verbatim. A preflight that surfaces records the authoritative fold treats as nonexistent creates a second, louder account of the same resolution rule with no declared edge; dc-4 justifies it as free disclosure that changes no verdict, but does not flag the tension with dc-3's single-authority framing (dc-3 is worded around sync, so the preflight's applicability is a judgment call nobody declared).\",\"confidence\":0.15,\"target\":\"spec/closure-ergonomics\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dcj-render-split-vs-one-code-path** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-2 commits the preflight to rendering richer detail (per-AC attestation paths, derived-tree roots, excluded-commit names) directly from evidence.StoryResult/FeatureResult — detail that a real close's refusal path never prints, since close surfaces only the gate functions' coarse Reason strings. Parent dc-5 states "the preflight report and close's refusal are one code path and their required agreement (co-3) is structural." Under dc-2's design the shared code path covers verdict computation only; the report and the refusal become two rendering paths whose textual content is guaranteed to differ, so co-3's agreement is structural at the verdict layer but not at the report layer, and preflight-only detail can drift from what a refusal would actually say. dc-2 discloses this as "a rendering difference, not a second verdict-computing implementation" but — unlike the dc-3 widening and the dc-2 ritual-step narrowing, which are explicitly flagged as tensions for seam adjudication — never names it as a deviation from dc-5's one-code-path wording, and no supersedes/exempts edge exists against closure-ergonomics#dc-5. (confidence 0.30)
- **judged-dcj-excluded-record-disclosure-vs-fold-authority** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-4 has the preflight explicitly name found-but-excluded (non-ancestor) commit directories, while the fold itself (records.go:90-96) silently drops them — parent dc-3 establishes that where two surfaces would disagree about record acceptance, "the fold's rule is authoritative" and other surfaces conform to it verbatim. A preflight that surfaces records the authoritative fold treats as nonexistent creates a second, louder account of the same resolution rule with no declared edge; dc-4 justifies it as free disclosure that changes no verdict, but does not flag the tension with dc-3's single-authority framing (dc-3 is worded around sync, so the preflight's applicability is a judgment call nobody declared). (confidence 0.15)
